stdout:
# Market Research: Text Syndicate — Impression/Affiliate-Driving Niches for Twitter/Threads/note.com

Per the role contract, this is a report only — I have not written or modified any repository files. The execution adapter is expected to persist this to `platform/system/runtime/business_agents/text_syndicate/market_research/task-0001/latest.{json,md}`.

## Facts (grounded in search results)

1. **X (Twitter) impression-based monetization has a real eligibility bar, not just "post and earn."** Creator Revenue Sharing requires an active X Premium subscription, ≥5,000,000 Verified Home Timeline impressions in the trailing 3 months, and ≥500 verified (paying) followers, with a $30 minimum payout threshold. ([xpayoutcalculator.com](https://www.xpayoutcalculator.com/eligibility/), [X Help](https://help.x.com/en/using-x/creator-revenue-sharing))
2. **Threads has no native impression/ad-revenue-sharing program in 2026.** Meta's 2024 invite-only bonus program wound down around April 2025 and is closed to new creators; Threads creator earnings today come entirely from off-platform sources (affiliate links, brand deals, funnels to owned assets). ([threadstomillions.com](https://threadstomillions.com/blog/threads-monetization-requirements))
3. **X algorithm (2026) rewards reply-driven, link-free posts, and treats engagement ratio, not follower count, as the gate for reach** — replies are weighted far above likes (cited as ~27-150x depending on source), and posts with external URLs in the body see roughly 30-50% reach suppression vs. equivalent link-free posts. Small/new accounts with high engagement ratio get strong distribution. ([Sprout Social](https://sproutsocial.com/insights/twitter-algorithm/), [OpenTweet](https://opentweet.io/blog/how-twitter-x-algorithm-works-2026))
4. **Threads algorithm (2026) explicitly favors new creators during an early growth window** and rewards niche focus, reply quality, and 30-60 minute engagement velocity over broad/generic posting. Recommended posting cadence cited is 2-3x/day. ([teract.ai](https://www.teract.ai/resources/grow-threads-following-2026))
5. **On X, the niches most associated with affiliate conversion are AI tools, SaaS, and finance** — attributed to recurring-revenue commission structures (SaaS affiliate programs cited at 20-70% recurring) and an audience skew toward entrepreneurs/marketers/developers. Long-form threads (X supports up to 25,000 characters) are cited as ranking in on-platform/search results and driving affiliate CTR. ([EarnifyHub](https://earnifyhub.com/blog/affiliate/x-twitter-affiliate-marketing-2026), [ucompares.com](https://ucompares.com/social-media/twitter/smart-ways-to-grow-affiliate-income/))
6. **note.com's affiliate rules have loosened.** Historically restricted to Amazon Associates only; multiple 2025-2026 Japanese-language sources report that external ASP links (Rakuten Affiliate, A8.net) are now generally permitted, provided sponsored/affiliate content is disclosed (PR/広告/アフィリエイトリンクを含みます) to avoid being treated as undisclosed advertising (ステマ). Sources disagree on exact current wording of note's terms, so this should be verified against note's live guidelines before committing content strategy to non-Amazon ASPs. ([mobinc.jp](https://mobinc.jp/column/2026/03/04/start-note-affiliate-and-monetize-guide-amazon-rakuten-use-and-rules/), [note.com/shichaoji](https://note.com/shichaoji/n/n0f1dbbe26e32))
7. **note.com content genres cited as currently growing (2026, Japanese source)**: book summaries/reviews in report format (esp. business/technical books), skill-up content for specific tools (Figma, Canva — cited as strong for search inflow), personal finance literacy (NISA, iDeCo), and side-hustle/income-disclosure/career-change content, which is described as pairing well with paid membership tiers. One source explicitly recommends the "AI × side-hustle × note" combination. ([note.com/yutori_kun01](https://note.com/yutori_kun01/n/n7b911f0b0911))

## Assumptions (not directly evidenced, inferred from business context)

- The business is open to publishing in Japanese given note.com is a Japanese-dominant platform and the other active business in this repo (Kabukicho Survival Map) is Japan/Tokyo-focused — this is an inference from platform choice and sibling-business pattern, not a stated fact. Needs explicit confirmation from the operator; if the intent is English-only, note.com is a weaker fit than Twitter/Threads.
- "Monetized via impressions" in the business notes was assumed by the operator to be achievable in the near term on all three platforms. Fact #1/#2 above show this assumption doesn't hold uniformly — it's realistic (with a real bar) on X, and not available at all natively on Threads.

## Hypotheses (untested, would need pilot data)

- A single core niche (e.g., AI tools/productivity, or personal-finance literacy) cross-posted natively (not just repurposed verbatim) across all three platforms would outperform a generalist account, because all three platforms' 2026 algorithms independently reward niche focus and penalize link-heavy generic content.
- Given X's reach penalty on posts with external links, an affiliate-thread strategy (long-form thread ending in one disclosed link, rather than link-in-every-post) would outperform per-post affiliate linking, but this hasn't been measured against this specific account's audience.

## Recommendations

1. **Do not rely on X's Creator Revenue Sharing as a near-term revenue path.** 5M impressions/3mo + 500 verified followers + a paid X Premium subscription is a high bar for a zero-audience launch. Treat X as an affiliate-link and audience-building channel first; revisit ad-revenue eligibility as a lagging KPI, not a launch goal.
2. **Do not budget for Threads impression/ad revenue at all** — it doesn't exist for creators in 2026. Use Threads purely as an affiliate-link and traffic-to-note.com channel, leaning into its cited early-account reach advantage.
3. **Pick one niche for the pilot, not three separate ones per platform.** Given zero-cost-to-produce text content and the shared "AI tools/SaaS/finance" affiliate strength on X plus the "AI × side-hustle" note.com trend, an AI-tools-for-productivity or side-hustle/personal-finance niche has evidence of both platform algorithmic favor and affiliate commission structure (recurring SaaS commissions) on at least two of the three platforms.
4. **Format posts to match each platform's 2026 ranking signals**: on X, keep affiliate links out of the main post body (put them in a reply or bio-link, since in-post links suppress reach 30-50%) and prioritize reply-bait phrasing; on Threads, post 2-3x/day and prioritize niche-specific replies to build the engagement-velocity signal; on note.com, favor long-form report/review formats (book summaries, tool walkthroughs) which are cited as the strongest current search-inflow genre.
5. **Verify note.com's current affiliate ASP policy directly (not via secondary blog sources) before committing to a non-Amazon affiliate network**, since sources disagree on exact current terms — this is a compliance risk, not just a strategy choice.
6. **Treat this as a validation experiment, not a committed strategy**: none of the above is proven for an audience-zero account; recommend a bounded pilot (e.g., 2-4 weeks, one niche, tracked impressions/replies/click-through) before scaling content production.
